name: The First State Bank
description_short: Community bank in Ness City, Kansas offering checking, savings, CDs, and internet banking with over 100 years of local ownership and FDIC insurance.
description_long:
First State Bank was organized in Beeler, Kansas in 1916 and relocated to Ness City in 1933, making it a century-old institution rooted in Ness County's financial landscape. The bank positions itself as a locally owned and operated financial institution providing agricultural, commercial, and consumer credit alongside deposit products and financial advisory services.

The bank's core offerings include checking accounts, savings accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs). Customers can access 24-hour internet banking for account management, fund transfers, loan payments, and transaction history viewing. The bank also provides bill payment services, debit cards, and online statements. All deposits are FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

First State Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on hometown personalized service and deep community integration spanning over a century. The bank offers free internet banking, mobile deposit functionality through a mobile app, and convenient access via a single physical branch location at 206 North Pennsylvania Avenue in Ness City, Kansas. Their marketing emphasizes relationships and local decision-making rather than corporate scale.

As a single-location community bank, First State Bank is best suited for customers prioritizing local relationships and personal service over digital-first banking or extensive branch networks. The limited online information about specific account terms, interest rates, and fee structures may require direct contact to make informed decisions. This institution serves primarily rural Kansas customers with agricultural and commercial banking needs alongside consumer services.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Free 24-hour internet banking with fund transfers and bill payment capabilities
Mobile app with mobile deposit functionality for remote check deposits
Over 100 years of continuous operation demonstrating stability and community commitment
Locally owned and operated with personal service emphasis from hometown staff
Multiple account types including checking, savings, and certificates of deposit
Online statements and demo access available for account management review
cons: Single branch location in Ness City, Kansas limits physical access for non-local customers
Website contains minimal information on interest rates, APYs, or specific fee structures requiring direct contact
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advanced financial products beyond basic banking
Limited digital marketing presence and outdated website design may indicate slower adoption of modern fintech features

contact.address: 206 N Pennsylvania Ave, Ness City, KS 67560

best_for: Agricultural and commercial customers in Ness County, Kansas seeking relationship-based banking
Rural Kansas residents prioritizing local ownership and personalized service over large corporate banks
Customers with existing community ties in Ness City wanting FDIC-protected deposit products
Small business owners in the region needing traditional commercial and agricultural credit
diagnosis: First State Bank is best for rural Kansas customers and agricultural/commercial businesses in Ness County who value century-old community relationships and personalized local service over national bank convenience or competitive digital features. The main caveat is that as a single-branch institution, it lacks geographic accessibility for non-local customers and provides limited transparent pricing information online, requiring phone contact or in-person visits to evaluate account terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 206 N Pennsylvania Ave, Ness City, KS 67560
